/cwe/weakness/622.Name: Improper Validation of Function Hook Arguments
/cwe/weakness/622.Description: The product adds hooks to user-accessible API functions, but it does not properly validate the arguments. This could lead to resultant vulnerabilities.
/cwe/weakness/622.ExtendedDescription: Such hooks can be used in defensive software that runs with privileges, such as anti-virus or firewall, which hooks kernel calls. When the arguments are not validated, they could be used to bypass the protection scheme or attack the product itself.

PotentialMitigations.Description: Ensure that all arguments are verified, as defined by the API you are protecting.
Drop privileges before invoking such functions, if possible.
